Today is February 9, 2026. Ghislaine Maxwell appeared virtually for a closed-door deposition from a Texas prison. According to her lawyer, David Oscar Markus, under what specific condition is Maxwell prepared to "speak fully and honestly"?
(A) If she is granted clemency by President Trump
(B) If she is granted legal immunity by the House Oversight Committee
(C) If the Department of Justice agrees to redact the names of 25 co-conspirators
(D) If she is allowed to meet with Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche again
(E) If the Supreme Court issues a favorable ruling regarding her 20-year sentence
explanation: The article states that Maxwell's lawyer, David Oscar Markus, posted on social media that Maxwell was "prepared to speak fully and honestly if granted clemency by President Trump."
model_output:
Based on the web search results from February 9, 2026, Ghislaine Maxwell's lawyer, David Oscar Markus, stated during her closed-door deposition that she is prepared to "speak fully and honestly" only if granted clemency by President Trump[[1]](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/09/ghislaine-maxwell-congress-deposition)[[2]](https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-02-10/ghislaine-maxwell-will-only-talk-if-trump-grants-clemency/106324720)[[3]](https://www.detroitnews.com/story/news/politics/2026/02/09/ghislaine-maxwell-declines-to-answer-lawmakers-questions-in-closed-door-deposition/88591024007). This directly matches option (A). None of the other options align with the reported statements from Markus.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/09/ghislaine-maxwell-congress-deposition)
- ABC News (https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-02-10/ghislaine-maxwell-will-only-talk-if-trump-grants-clemency/106324720)
- Detroit News (https://www.detroitnews.com/story/news/politics/2026/02/09/ghislaine-maxwell-declines-to-answer-lawmakers-questions-in-closed-door-deposition/88591024007)

Today is February 9, 2026. The US military recently boarded an oil tanker named Aquila II in the Indian Ocean. According to the Department of Defense, what was the specific reason for tracking and boarding this vessel?
(A) It was suspected of transporting narcotics from the Caribbean to the Indian Ocean
(B) It was operating in defiance of a US "quarantine of sanctioned vessels in the Caribbean"
(C) It was carrying Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro following his removal from power
(D) It was a vessel associated with Chevron that failed to report its cargo to US authorities
(E) It was identified as a "hidden tranche" of information related to the Epstein investigation
explanation: The Department of Defense stated the ship was operating in defiance of a US "quarantine of sanctioned vessels in the Caribbean."
model_output:
The search results indicate that the US Department of Defense (via statements from the Pentagon and Defense Secretary) cited the Aquila II's defiance of a US quarantine on sanctioned vessels in the Caribbean as the reason for tracking and boarding it. Some reports note that the vessel was also linked to Venezuelan or Russian oil shipments, but the quarantine defiance is the specific rationale provided.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 9, 2026. The Seattle Seahawks won Super Bowl 60 on Sunday. What specific medical detail is provided regarding the birth of Seahawks linebacker Derick Hall?
(A) He was born weighing 6lb 3in and had a 5% chance of survival
(B) He was born three months premature and weighed exactly 3lb
(C) He weighed less than 3lb and was on life support when his mother refused to have it discontinued
(D) He was born in Santa Clara, California, with a 1% chance of survival
(E) He was born weighing 18st 2lb, which required immediate life support
explanation: The article mentions that Derick Hall was born four months premature in 2001, weighed less than 3lb, and was on life support when his mother refused to have it discontinued.
model_output:
The story of Derick Hall's birth has been widely shared, especially in light of the Seahawks' Super Bowl 60 victory. Based on available details, he was born at 23.5 weeks (four months premature) in Gulfport, Mississippi, weighing 2 pounds 9 ounces, with no heartbeat and a brain bleed. Doctors gave him only a 1% chance of survival, and his mother refused to sign a DNR form, effectively fighting to keep him on life support.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Derick Hall One Percent Foundation](https://dhallonepercent.org/about)[[1]](https://dhallonepercent.org/about)

Today is February 9, 2026. White House aide Stephen Miller has faced criticism following the shooting of Alex Pretti in Minneapolis. What was Miller's rare "walk-back" statement regarding the administration's initial assessment of the incident?
(A) He admitted that Pretti was a licensed handgun owner and not a domestic terrorist
(B) He stated that the Customs and Border Patrol officials had acted in self-defense after being threatened
(C) He claimed that the video evidence of the shooting had been manipulated by Democratic critics
(D) He announced that the administration would now adopt a "softer touch" in immigration enforcement
(E) He contended that the initial assessment was based on reports from "on the ground" officers who may not have been following proper protocol
explanation: Miller provided a statement to the media contending that the administration's initial assessment was "based on reports" from "on the ground" immigration officers—and that those agents "may not have been following" proper protocol.
model_output:
The incident involving the shooting of Alex Pretti by federal agents in Minneapolis appears to have occurred in late January 2026, based on news reports. White House aide Stephen Miller initially labeled Pretti a "domestic terrorist" and "would-be assassin" shortly after the event. Facing backlash, Miller later issued a rare walk-back statement acknowledging that Customs and Border Protection (CBP) agents may have violated protocol. Specifically, he noted that the White House had provided guidance to DHS on using extra personnel for force protection and fugitive operations, and they were evaluating why the CBP team might not have followed that protocol. This aligns with reports of a blame game, where the initial assessment was attributed to potentially inaccurate reports from on-the-ground officers.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [The New York Times] (https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/28/us/stephen-miller-alex-pretti-shooting.html)[[1]](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/28/us/stephen-miller-alex-pretti-shooting.html)
- [CNN] (https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/27/politics/stephen-miller-alex-pretti-trump)[[2]](https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/27/politics/stephen-miller-alex-pretti-trump)
- [Fox Baltimore] (https://foxbaltimore.com/news/nation-world/stephen-miller-admits-cbp-may-have-violated-protocol-in-fatal-alex-pretti-shooting)[[3]](https://foxbaltimore.com/news/nation-world/stephen-miller-admits-cbp-may-have-violated-protocol-in-fatal-alex-pretti-shooting)
- [Axios] (https://www.axios.com/2026/01/27/trump-stephen-miller-massacre-minnesota-shooting)[[4]](https://www.axios.com/2026/01/27/trump-stephen-miller-massacre-minnesota-shooting)

Today is February 9, 2026. US news anchor Savannah Guthrie has pleaded for the return of her mother, Nancy Guthrie. What specific detail did authorities release regarding Nancy Guthrie's pacemaker?
(A) The pacemaker itself was found disconnected and removed from her home
(B) The app connected to the device was disconnected from her phone at about 02:28 local time
(C) The device sent a distress signal to the Pima County sheriff's office at 02:28 local time
(D) The pacemaker was found to be missing its necessary life-saving medication
(E) The device was used by the FBI to track her location to a house in Tucson
explanation: Authorities stated that at about 02:28 local time on the morning she went missing, "the app connected to her pacemaker, an implanted cardiac device, was disconnected from her phone."
model_output:
Based on recent news reports about the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, the key detail released by authorities matches option (B). The pacemaker stopped syncing (i.e., disconnected) from associated apps on her Apple devices around 2:28 a.m. local time on February 1, 2026. No other options align with the reported facts.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

Today is February 9, 2026. Bad Bunny performed at the Super Bowl in a beige sweater emblazoned with the number 64. According to the article, what is the potential significance of this number?
(A) It represents the number of Spanish-speaking nations in the Americas
(B) It is the number of Grammy awards Bad Bunny has won in his career
(C) It could represent the official death toll of the 2017 Hurricane Maria
(D) It represents the number of days Liam Conejo Ramos was detained by ICE
(E) It is the street number of the "casita" where Bad Bunny grew up in Puerto Rico
explanation: The article notes that the number 64 "could represent the official death toll [of Hurricane Maria], which turned out to be significantly lower than the estimated thousands who died."
model_output:
The number 64 on Bad Bunny's sweater during his 2026 Super Bowl performance has sparked various theories across articles. Common speculations include a nod to his mother's birth year (1964), a tribute to a late uncle's football jersey number, or a reference to his Grammy history (like the 64th Grammy Awards). However, multiple sources also highlight a potential connection to the initial official death toll from Hurricane Maria in 2017, which devastated Puerto Rico—Bad Bunny's homeland—and has been a recurring theme in his activism and music.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

(C) It became the most-watched non-English show on HBO Max in Australia

explanation: The article states: "Its penultimate episode has broken records to tie with Breaking Bad for the highest-rated episode ever on entertainment website IMDB."
model_output:
I reviewed available information about the TV show "Heated Rivalry," which is a Canadian series that premiered in late 2025 and gained significant popularity. Based on multiple sources, its first season has six episodes, making Episode 5 ("I'll Believe in Anything") the penultimate one. This episode is specifically noted for tying with Breaking Bad's "Ozymandias" as the highest-rated TV episode ever on IMDb (both at a 10/10 rating). No other records matching the provided options were mentioned for this episode.

<final_answer>
(A)

(A) The Coast Salish tribe near Tacoma, Washington
(B) The Kwakwaka'wakw tribe on Vancouver Island
(C) The Puyallup Tribe in Seattle, Washington
(D) The Hudson Nation in Maine
(E) The Port Alberni community in British Columbia
explanation: The article states that the "Seahawks logo was copied from the Kwakwaka'wakw tribe on Vancouver Island."
model_output:
The Seattle Seahawks logo was inspired by a transformation mask from the Kwakwaka'wakw people, specifically from the northeast side of Vancouver Island in British Columbia, Canada. While the mask is apparently now housed in a museum in Maine, its original cultural and geographic roots trace back to the Kwakwaka'wakw.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

(D) He made the infamous decision to throw on the one-yard line 11 years ago

explanation: The article states: "Mike Vrabel can become the first man to win a Super Bowl as head coach and player with the same team."
model_output:
I am considering the query about Super Bowl 60 on February 9, 2026, with Mike Vrabel as the Patriots' head coach. This appears to be a hypothetical scenario based on the given date, but I'll evaluate the options using historical facts and recent search results projected to 2026.

Mike Vrabel won three Super Bowls as a player with the Patriots (XXXVI, XXXVIII, XXXIX). In this scenario, he's their head coach. None of the search results indicate he's an overseas coach (A), coached at Baltimore (B), won 10 Super Bowls as a coach (C), or made the infamous goal-line throw decision in Super Bowl 49 (D—that was Seahawks coach Pete Carroll in 2015, 11 years prior).

However, multiple sources confirm that if Vrabel wins Super Bowl 60, he'd be the first to win a Super Bowl as both a player and head coach with the same team (E). Others have won in both roles but not with the same franchise.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
